title: Humorous Masterpieces from American Literature
summaries: "Humorous Masterpieces from American Literature" by Edward T. Mason is a collection of humorous literary works edited in the late 19th century. This anthology features a variety of comedic pieces from notable American authors, showcasing their wit and comedic storytelling. The collection likely explores themes of human nature, society, and the absurdities of everyday life, entertaining readers with clever observations and relatable situations.  The opening of the collection presents an engaging selection from "Selections from the Experiences of the A.C." by Bayard Taylor, where Mr. Johnson arrives in Waterbury to meet his old friend, Mr. Billings. Their conversation quickly drifts into nostalgia as they reminisce about the past and discuss their youthful circle, humorously reflecting on sentiments of the Arcadian Club. As they delve into amusing anecdotes, including the quirks of their social companions, the tone becomes light-hearted, setting the stage for the humorous explorations that will continue throughout the anthology. The narrative hints at the absurdity of their interactions and the social pretensions of their youth, promising a delightful blend of humor and commentary on human foibles.
